← All IRC handicap examples

IRC under A5.3 — DNF uses starters+1, DNC keeps entries+1

RRS A5.3 is the alternative penalty rule: codes whose penalty base is 'starters' (DNF, DNS, OCS, RET, DSQ, BFD, UFD…) score the number of boats that came to the starting area + 1 instead of series entries + 1. Only DNC keeps the 'entries' base (entries + 1) — a boat that never came to the starting area. The setting applies equally to scratch and handicap fleets.

Scoring system: IRC 
Fleet size = 5 (entries-base penalty = 6).
Check-in data is recorded (startPresent set on every row).
Boats present at the line: 1, 2, 3, 4 → starters = 4 (starters-base penalty = 5).
E (Echo) was absent → DNC.

Start 14:00:00

Boat 1 (TCC 1.0): finish 14:50:00  ET = 3000  CT = 3000.0  → 1st  (1 pt)
Boat 2 (TCC 1.0): finish 15:00:00  ET = 3600  CT = 3600.0  → 2nd  (2 pt)
Boat 3 (TCC 1.0): finish 15:10:00  ET = 4200  CT = 4200.0  → 3rd  (3 pt)
Boat 4 (TCC 1.0): DNF                                       → 5 pt (starters+1, A5.3)
Boat 5 (TCC 1.0): DNC                                       → 6 pt (entries+1, unchanged)

Race arithmetic

Gun time: 14:00:00
RankNameSail #TCCFinish timeETTCFCTPoints
1 Alpha 1 1.000 14:50:00 50m 00s 1.0000 50m 00s 1
2 Bravo 2 1.000 15:00:00 60m 00s 1.0000 60m 00s 2
3 Charlie 3 1.000 15:10:00 70m 00s 1.0000 70m 00s 3
— Delta 4 1.000 DNF — 1.0000 — 6
— Echo 5 1.000 DNC — 1.0000 — 6

Series standings

RankNameSail #R1TotalNet
1 Alpha 1 1 1 1
2 Bravo 2 2 2 2
3 Charlie 3 3 3 3
4 Delta 4 DNF 5 5
5 Echo 5 DNC 6 6
sailscoring.ie
